questions.questionText: What is the primary purpose of using the 'use strict' directive in JavaScript?
options: To enable the use of deprecated functions
To disable the use of strict mode in JavaScript
To throw more exceptions and improve error handling
To enforce stricter syntax and runtime checks
questions.correctAnswer: To enforce stricter syntax and runtime checks
questions.userAnswer: To throw more exceptions and improve error handling

feedback.betterAnswer: The 'use strict' directive in JavaScript is used to enforce stricter syntax and runtime checks, helping developers catch common coding mistakes and improve the overall quality of their code.

questions.questionText: What is the main difference between the 'null' and 'undefined' values in JavaScript?
options: Null is a primitive value and undefined is an object
Null represents the absence of a value, while undefined represents an uninitialized variable
Null is used for numeric variables and undefined is used for string variables
Null is used for string variables and undefined is used for numeric variables
questions.correctAnswer: Null represents the absence of a value, while undefined represents an uninitialized variable

feedback.betterAnswer: In JavaScript, null is a primitive value that represents the absence of a value, whereas undefined is a type that represents an uninitialized variable or a variable that has not been declared.

questions.questionText: What is the primary purpose of the 'defer' attribute in a script tag?
options: To specify the script language
To prevent the script from running until the page has finished loading
To enable asynchronous script loading
To link an external stylesheet
questions.correctAnswer: To prevent the script from running until the page has finished loading
questions.userAnswer: To prevent the script from running until the page has finished loading

feedback.betterAnswer: The 'defer' attribute is used to specify that the script should not be executed until the page has finished loading. This can help improve page load times and prevent scripts from interfering with the rendering of the page.

questions.questionText: What is the main difference between the 'null' and 'undefined' values in JavaScript?
options: Null is a primitive value and undefined is an object
Null represents the absence of a value and undefined represents an uninitialized variable
Null is used for numeric variables and undefined is used for string variables
Null is used for arrays and undefined is used for objects
questions.correctAnswer: Null represents the absence of a value and undefined represents an uninitialized variable
questions.userAnswer: Null represents the absence of a value and undefined represents an uninitialized variable

feedback.betterAnswer: In JavaScript, null represents the intentional absence of any object value, while undefined represents an uninitialized variable or a variable that has not been declared.

questions.questionText: What is the primary function of the 'use strict' directive in JavaScript?
options: To enable strict mode for the entire script
To disable strict mode for the entire script
To allow the use of deprecated JavaScript features
To optimize JavaScript code for better performance
questions.correctAnswer: To enable strict mode for the entire script
questions.userAnswer: To allow the use of deprecated JavaScript features

feedback.betterAnswer: The 'use strict' directive is used to enable strict mode in JavaScript, which helps to catch common coding mistakes and improves the overall security and quality of the code.

questions.questionText: What is the primary purpose of using the 'bind' method in JavaScript?
options: To change the scope of a function to the global object
To change the scope of a function to a specified object
To invoke a function immediately with a specified scope
To create a new function with the same behavior as the original function
questions.correctAnswer: To change the scope of a function to a specified object

feedback.betterAnswer: The 'bind' method in JavaScript is used to set the context of a function to a specified object. It returns a new function that has its 'this' keyword set to the provided value, with a given sequence of arguments preceding any provided when the new function is called.
